prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene that explains how a tangent slope changes along a curve. Show axes, a smooth cubic graph, a moving probe dot, and a tangent line that updates as the probe slides from left to right.
completion:
from manim import *

class TangentSlopeProbe(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Changing Tangent Slope", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-2.5, 2.5, 1],
            y_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            x_length=7,
            y_length=4.5,
            tips=False,
        ).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        axis_labels = axes.get_axis_labels(Text("x", font_size=22), Text("f(x)", font_size=22))

        def curve(x):
            return 0.45 * x**3 - 1.2 * x

        def slope(x):
            return 1.35 * x**2 - 1.2

        graph = axes.plot(curve, x_range=[-2.2, 2.2], color=BLUE_D)
        tracker = ValueTracker(-1.7)
        probe_dot = always_redraw(
            lambda: Dot(axes.c2p(tracker.get_value(), curve(tracker.get_value())), color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.07)
        )
        tangent_line = always_redraw(
            lambda: axes.plot(
                lambda u: slope(tracker.get_value()) * (u - tracker.get_value()) + curve(tracker.get_value()),
                x_range=[tracker.get_value() - 0.75, tracker.get_value() + 0.75],
                color=GREEN_D,
            )
        )
        slope_readout = always_redraw(
            lambda: VGroup(
                Text("slope", font_size=22, color=GRAY_C),
                DecimalNumber(slope(tracker.get_value()), num_decimal_places=2, font_size=24, color=GREEN_D),
            ).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.12).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN * 0.4)
        )
        caption = Text("The tangent gets steeper or flatter depending on where we probe.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), FadeIn(axis_labels), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(graph), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(probe_dot), Create(tangent_line), FadeIn(slope_readout), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(1.8), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), run_time=1)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that shows the fourth roots of unity on the complex plane. Draw the unit circle, place four labeled roots, connect them into a square, and emphasize that each root is evenly spaced by ninety degrees.
completion:
from manim import *

class ComplexRootsPolygonStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Fourth Roots of Unity", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = ComplexPlane(
            x_range=[-2.5, 2.5, 1],
            y_range=[-2.5, 2.5, 1],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=6,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.25},
        ).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        plane_labels = plane.get_axis_labels(Text("Re", font_size=22), Text("Im", font_size=22))
        center = plane.n2p(0)
        unit_circle = Circle(radius=plane.get_x_unit_size(), color=BLUE_D).move_to(center)
        root_values = [1, 1j, -1, -1j]
        directions = [RIGHT, UP, LEFT, DOWN]
        point_group = VGroup()
        label_group = VGroup()
        for index, (value, direction) in enumerate(zip(root_values, directions), start=1):
            point = Dot(plane.n2p(value), color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.08)
            label = Text(f"z{index}", font_size=22, color=GRAY_C).next_to(point, direction, buff=0.18)
            point_group.add(point)
            label_group.add(label)
        polygon = Polygon(*[point.get_center() for point in point_group], color=GREEN_D)
        radii = VGroup(*[Line(center, point.get_center(), color=GRAY_B) for point in point_group])
        angle_note = Text("Every root is separated by 90 degrees.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), FadeIn(plane_labels), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(unit_circle), run_time=1)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[Create(radius) for radius in radii], lag_ratio=0.2), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(point) for point in point_group], lag_ratio=0.2), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(label) for label in label_group], lag_ratio=0.2), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(polygon), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(angle_note), Indicate(point_group, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 15-second Manim scene that explains binary search on a sorted list. Show a row of numbered boxes, a highlighted search interval, a moving middle pointer, and two interval updates before the target is found.
completion:
from manim import *

class BinarySearchIntervalStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Binary Search", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        values = [4, 7, 11, 14, 18, 21, 26, 30]
        cells = VGroup()
        for value in values:
            box = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.08, width=0.9, height=0.62, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_E, fill_opacity=0.14)
            label = Integer(value, font_size=24).move_to(box)
            cells.add(VGroup(box, label))
        cells.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.16).shift(DOWN * 0.4)
        target_label = Text("target = 26", font_size=26, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(cells, UP, buff=0.45)
        pointer = Triangle(fill_color=YELLOW_D, fill_opacity=1, stroke_width=0).scale(0.14).rotate(PI).next_to(cells[3], UP, buff=0.12)

        interval_one = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(*cells[:]), color=GREEN_D, buff=0.08)
        interval_two = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(*cells[4:]), color=GREEN_D, buff=0.08)
        interval_three = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(*cells[6:7]), color=GREEN_D, buff=0.08)
        mid_notes = VGroup(
            Text("14 is too small", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN),
            Text("21 is too small", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN),
            Text("26 found", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN),
        )
        found_box = SurroundingRectangle(cells[6], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.1)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(cells), FadeIn(target_label), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(interval_one), FadeIn(pointer), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(mid_notes[0]), run_time=0.8)
        self.play(pointer.animate.next_to(cells[5], UP, buff=0.12), ReplacementTransform(interval_one, interval_two), run_time=2)
        self.play(Transform(mid_notes[0], mid_notes[1]), run_time=0.8)
        self.play(pointer.animate.next_to(cells[6], UP, buff=0.12), ReplacementTransform(interval_two, interval_three), run_time=2)
        self.play(Transform(mid_notes[0], mid_notes[2]), Create(found_box), Indicate(cells[6], color=YELLOW_A), run_time=2.5)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 15-second Manim scene that explains image formation with a convex lens. Show the principal axis, a vertical lens, focal points on both sides, an upright object on the left, two principal rays, and the inverted image on the right.
completion:
from manim import *

class ConvexLensRayStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Convex Lens Ray Diagram", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        axis = DashedLine(LEFT * 6, RIGHT * 6, color=GRAY_C).shift(DOWN * 0.3)
        lens = Line(UP * 2.1, DOWN * 2.1, color=BLUE_D, stroke_width=6).shift(DOWN * 0.3)
        lens_label = Text("lens", font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).next_to(lens, UP, buff=0.15)
        left_focus = Dot(LEFT * 2.4 + DOWN * 0.3, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.07)
        right_focus = Dot(RIGHT * 2.4 + DOWN * 0.3, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.07)
        focus_labels = VGroup(
            Text("F", font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(left_focus, DOWN, buff=0.12),
            Text("F", font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(right_focus, DOWN, buff=0.12),
        )
        object_arrow = Arrow(LEFT * 4.7 + DOWN * 1.3, LEFT * 4.7 + UP * 1.1, buff=0, color=GREEN_D)
        object_label = Text("object", font_size=22, color=GREEN_D).next_to(object_arrow, LEFT, buff=0.15)
        image_arrow = Arrow(RIGHT * 4.0 + UP * 0.8, RIGHT * 4.0 + DOWN * 1.7, buff=0, color=TEAL_D)
        image_label = Text("image", font_size=22, color=TEAL_D).next_to(image_arrow, RIGHT, buff=0.15)
        ray_one_a = Line(object_arrow.get_end(), lens.get_center() + UP * 1.1, color=ORANGE)
        ray_one_b = Line(lens.get_center() + UP * 1.1, image_arrow.get_end(), color=ORANGE)
        ray_two_a = Line(object_arrow.get_end(), left_focus.get_center(), color=RED_D)
        ray_two_b = Line(lens.get_center(), image_arrow.get_end(), color=RED_D)
        caption = Text("A ray through the lens center stays straight, while a parallel ray bends through the focus.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axis), Create(lens), FadeIn(lens_label), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(left_focus), FadeIn(right_focus), FadeIn(focus_labels), FadeIn(object_arrow), FadeIn(object_label), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(ray_one_a), Create(ray_one_b), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(ray_two_a), Create(ray_two_b), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(image_arrow), FadeIn(image_label), FadeIn(caption), run_time=2)
        self.play(Indicate(image_arrow, color=TEAL_A), run_time=1)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains gradient descent on a one-dimensional loss curve. Show a parabola-like loss surface, several parameter updates moving downhill toward the minimum, and a caption that each step follows the local slope.
completion:
from manim import *

class GradientDescentStepTrace(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Gradient Descent Steps", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-2.5, 4.5, 1],
            y_range=[0, 5, 1],
            x_length=7,
            y_length=4.5,
            tips=False,
        ).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        axis_labels = axes.get_axis_labels(Text("parameter", font_size=22), Text("loss", font_size=22))
        loss_curve = axes.plot(lambda x: 0.48 * (x - 1.2) ** 2 + 0.7, x_range=[-2.0, 4.0], color=BLUE_D)
        x_positions = [-1.6, -0.5, 0.3, 0.8, 1.1]
        points = [Dot(axes.c2p(x, 0.48 * (x - 1.2) ** 2 + 0.7), color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.07) for x in x_positions]
        point_group = VGroup(*points)
        arrows = VGroup(*[
            Arrow(points[i].get_center(), points[i + 1].get_center(), buff=0.08, color=GREEN_D, stroke_width=4)
            for i in range(len(points) - 1)
        ])
        basin = SurroundingRectangle(point_group[-2:], color=TEAL_D, buff=0.12)
        caption = Text("Each update moves opposite the local slope toward lower loss.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), FadeIn(axis_labels), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(loss_curve), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(point_group[0]), run_time=0.5)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[GrowArrow(arrow) for arrow in arrows], lag_ratio=0.2), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(dot) for dot in point_group[1:]], lag_ratio=0.2), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(basin), FadeIn(caption), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains a Bayes-style belief update with simple bars. Show a prior probability panel, an evidence arrow in the middle, and a posterior panel where the highlighted bar becomes much taller after the update.
completion:
from manim import *

class BayesBarUpdateStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Belief Update", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)

        def make_panel(header, positive_height, color):
            frame = Rectangle(width=2.3, height=3.1, color=GRAY_B)
            positive = Rectangle(width=0.8, height=positive_height, color=color, fill_color=color, fill_opacity=0.4, stroke_width=2).align_to(frame, DOWN).shift(LEFT * 0.45 + UP * 0.1)
            negative = Rectangle(width=0.8, height=2.6 - positive_height, color=BLUE_E, fill_color=BLUE_E, fill_opacity=0.2, stroke_width=2).next_to(positive, RIGHT, buff=0.2).align_to(positive, DOWN)
            labels = VGroup(
                Text(header, font_size=24).next_to(frame, UP, buff=0.18),
                Text("event", font_size=18, color=color).next_to(positive, DOWN, buff=0.12),
                Text("not event", font_size=18, color=BLUE_E).next_to(negative, DOWN, buff=0.12),
            )
            return VGroup(frame, positive, negative, labels)

        prior = make_panel("Prior", 0.65, ORANGE).shift(LEFT * 3.0 + DOWN * 0.1)
        posterior = make_panel("Posterior", 1.7, GREEN_D).shift(RIGHT * 3.0 + DOWN * 0.1)
        evidence_arrow = Arrow(LEFT * 1.0, RIGHT * 1.0, color=YELLOW_D, buff=0)
        evidence_text = Text("new evidence", font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(evidence_arrow, UP, buff=0.15)
        change_note = Text("The favored explanation gains much more probability after seeing informative evidence.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(prior), run_time=2)
        self.play(GrowArrow(evidence_arrow), FadeIn(evidence_text), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(TransformFromCopy(prior, posterior), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(change_note), Indicate(posterior[1], color=GREEN_A), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 14-second Manim scene that explains vector projection onto a line. Show a plane, a slanted reference line, an original vector, its projected component on the line, and a dashed residual segment connecting the vector tip to the projection.
completion:
from manim import *

class ProjectionResidualStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Vector Projection", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-4, 4, 1],
            y_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            x_length=7,
            y_length=5,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.18},
        ).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        line = Line(plane.c2p(-3.4, -1.7), plane.c2p(3.4, 1.7), color=BLUE_D)
        origin = plane.c2p(0, 0)
        vector_tip = plane.c2p(2.2, 2.0)
        projection_tip = plane.c2p(2.56, 1.28)
        vector = Arrow(origin, vector_tip, buff=0, color=YELLOW_D)
        projection = Arrow(origin, projection_tip, buff=0, color=GREEN_D)
        residual = DashedLine(projection_tip, vector_tip, color=RED_D)
        labels = VGroup(
            Text("reference line", font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).next_to(line, UL, buff=0.15),
            Text("vector", font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(vector_tip, UR, buff=0.12),
            Text("projection", font_size=22, color=GREEN_D).next_to(projection_tip, DOWN, buff=0.12),
            Text("residual", font_size=22, color=RED_D).next_to(residual, RIGHT, buff=0.12),
        )
        caption = Text("Projection keeps the component along the chosen line and leaves the rest as residual.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(line), FadeIn(labels[0]), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(GrowArrow(vector), FadeIn(labels[1]), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(GrowArrow(projection), FadeIn(labels[2]), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(Create(residual), FadeIn(labels[3]), FadeIn(caption), run_time=2)
        self.play(Indicate(projection, color=GREEN_A), run_time=1)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that gives intuition for sigma bond formation. Show two circular atomic orbitals approaching each other, highlight their overlap region, and add a short caption that head-on overlap concentrates electron density between the nuclei.
completion:
from manim import *

class SigmaBondOverlapStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Sigma Bond Overlap", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        left_orbital = Circle(radius=1.2, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_C, fill_opacity=0.22).shift(LEFT * 2.0 + DOWN * 0.2)
        right_orbital = Circle(radius=1.2, color=TEAL_D, fill_color=TEAL_C, fill_opacity=0.22).shift(RIGHT * 2.0 + DOWN * 0.2)
        left_nucleus = Dot(left_orbital.get_center(), color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.08)
        right_nucleus = Dot(right_orbital.get_center(), color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.08)
        nucleus_labels = VGroup(
            Text("nucleus", font_size=20, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(left_nucleus, DOWN, buff=0.14),
            Text("nucleus", font_size=20, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(right_nucleus, DOWN, buff=0.14),
        )
        overlap_label = Text("shared electron density", font_size=22, color=GREEN_D).next_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 0.2, UP, buff=1.25)
        caption = Text("Head-on overlap concentrates density between the nuclei.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(left_orbital), FadeIn(right_orbital), FadeIn(left_nucleus), FadeIn(right_nucleus), FadeIn(nucleus_labels), run_time=2)
        self.play(
            left_orbital.animate.shift(RIGHT * 0.9),
            right_orbital.animate.shift(LEFT * 0.9),
            left_nucleus.animate.move_to(left_orbital.get_center() + RIGHT * 0.9),
            right_nucleus.animate.move_to(right_orbital.get_center() + LEFT * 0.9),
            nucleus_labels[0].animate.next_to(left_orbital.get_center() + RIGHT * 0.9, DOWN, buff=1.05),
            nucleus_labels[1].animate.next_to(right_orbital.get_center() + LEFT * 0.9, DOWN, buff=1.05),
            run_time=3,
        )
        overlap = Intersection(
            left_orbital.copy(),
            right_orbital.copy(),
            color=GREEN_D,
            fill_color=GREEN_D,
            fill_opacity=0.35,
            stroke_width=0,
        )
        self.play(FadeIn(overlap), FadeIn(overlap_label), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), Indicate(overlap, color=GREEN_A), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)
